source: Zylos AI — Graceful Degradation Patterns in AI Agent Systems
summary: Production research showing 41–86.7% multi-agent failure rates without deliberate fault tolerance; defines circuit-breaker, retry, bulkhead, and fallback chain patterns for AI agent systems.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — ProcessRunner has no retry logic; any transient failure immediately triggers AIBlocked requiring human intervention

source: DEV Community — How to Build AI Agents That Fail Safely
summary: Practical guide to circuit breakers, health checks, and graceful degradation for AI agents in production; covers exponential backoff with jitter for retry policies.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — complementary source for HID-111; reinforces retry + circuit-breaker pattern for ProcessRunner

source: SYNAPSE — Empowering LLM Agents with Episodic-Semantic Memory via Spreading Activation
summary: Novel memory architecture modeling agent memory as a dynamic graph with spreading activation; lateral inhibition and temporal decay highlight relevant sub-graphs; outperforms SOTA on LoCoMo benchmark for complex temporal and multi-hop reasoning.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — AgentForge has no cross-run memory; SYNAPSE's spreading-activation boost maps cleanly onto the existing AgentSkill keyword retrieval pattern

source: Zylos AI — AI Agent Cost Optimization: Token Economics and FinOps in Production
summary: Production analysis showing 80%+ cost reduction achievable via model routing; frontier reasoning models can cost 190× more than small fast models for equivalent simple tasks; four pillars: cost landscape, caching, model routing, LLM FinOps.
relevanceToAgentForge: High — CLAUDE_CODE_MODEL is a single global setting; complexity-driven routing to haiku/sonnet/opus tiers could dramatically cut per-run cost

summary: Framework that predicts and pre-executes likely future tool calls during LLM thinking time; maintains active and shadow execution queues; achieves 48.5% reduction in task completion time and 1.8x tool throughput.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — PASTE operates at the intra-agent tool-call level, which AgentForge cannot control (Claude CLI handles its own tool calls internally). Indirect benefit only via reducing Claude session duration.

source: Speculative Actions: A Lossless Framework for Faster Agentic Systems (arxiv:2510.04371)
summary: Framework using speculative execution analogous to CPU branch prediction to hide tool latency; optimistic concurrency, discard on mispredict, no correctness impact.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — same architectural limitation as PASTE; Claude CLI manages its own tool execution, so AgentForge cannot inject speculative parallelism at the tool level.

source: Requesty Blog — 5 AI Agent Techniques May 2026 (IdleSpec, MOSS, Workflow Compilation)
summary: Weekly roundup covering: MOSS (agents that rewrite their own source code), workflow compilation (distilling multi-step pipelines into fine-tuned weights), and IdleSpec (speculative pre-planning between tool calls).
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — IdleSpec could pre-render next-stage prompts during agent execution; MOSS and workflow compilation are out of scope for CLI-subprocess architecture.

summary: Open-source ML experimentation loop: describe research directions in markdown, AI agent iterates train.py improvements, keeps only changes that beat the current best. Ran 700 experiments in 2 days, discovered 20 optimizations.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — inspiring pattern for self-improving orchestration but primarily targets ML experimentation loops, not issue-driven code generation pipelines.

summary: TypeScript-native framework launched April 2026; converts goals to task DAGs automatically; supports concurrent subtask execution with periodic plan-refinement synchronization.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — AgentForge's sequential state machine is a design choice, not a gap; DAG-based parallel execution would require a fundamental architecture rewrite.

source: Augment Code — 7 Best AI Agent Observability Tools 2026
summary: Survey of production AI agent observability tools including OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions, Langfuse, AgentOps; covers per-run token counts, costs, latency tracing.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — AgentForge has no cost/token tracking per run; OpenTelemetry integration could complement HID-113 model routing analytics, but would be a separate chore issue.

summary: 30 runnable Jupyter notebooks covering conversation buffers, vector stores, knowledge graphs, episodic/semantic memory, MemGPT, Mem0, Letta, Zep, Graphiti, LoCoMo benchmarks; comprehensive practical guide.
relevanceToAgentForge: Medium — good reference for HID-112 implementation; confirms keyword-based retrieval as a valid starting point before vector embeddings.

source: ICLR 2026 Workshop on AI with Recursive Self-Improvement
summary: Workshop summary on recursive AI self-improvement; identifies the verifiability constraint — self-improvement only works reliably in domains where outcomes are objectively verifiable; covers LLM agents rewriting their own codebases.
relevanceToAgentForge: Low — self-rewriting codebases require the agent to have write access to its own orchestration code, which is outside AgentForge's current safety model.
